RxNorm
alendronate 70 mg tablet
Take 1 tablet every week by oral route.
Caltrate 600-D Minerals 600 mg (carbonate)-800 unit-40 mg chew tablet
Take by oral route.
Estrace 0.01% (0.1 mg/gram) vaginal cream
Insert by vaginal route.
magnesium hydroxide 400 mg/5 mL oral suspension
Take 30 mL every day by oral route.
melatonin
5 MG
albuterol sulfate 2.5 mg/3 mL (0.083 %) solution for nebulization
INHALE 3 ML 3 TIMES A DAY BY NEBULIZATION ROUTE AS NEEDED FOR 60 DAYS, FOR WHEEZING ONLY.
trazodone 100 mg tablet
Take 1 tablet every day by oral route.
Allergy Relief (fexofenadine) 180 mg tablet
TAKE 1 TABLET BY MOUTH EVERY DAY
atorvastatin 20 mg tablet
TAKE 1 TABLET BY MOUTH EVERYDAY AT BEDTIME
benzonatate 200 mg capsule
TAKE 1 CAPSULE BY MOUTH THREE TIMES A DAY
fluticasone propionate 50 mcg/actuation nasal spray,suspension
2 SPRAYS IN EACH NOSTRIL ONCE DAILY, AT BEDTIME
latanoprost 0.005 % eye drops
PLACE 1 DROP IN BOTH EYES AT BEDTIME
mupirocin 2 % topical ointment
APPLY TO THE AFFECTED SITE TWICE DAILY FOR 2 WEEK
omeprazole 40 mg capsule,delayed release
TAKE 1 CAPSULE BY MOUTH EVERY DAY
trazodone 150 mg tablet
TAKE 1 TABLET BY MOUTH EVERYDAY AT BEDTIME
nitrofurantoin monohydrate/macrocrystals 100 mg capsule
TAKE 1 CAPSULE BY MOUTH TWICE DAILY FOR 5 DAYS
solifenacin 5 mg tablet
TAKE 1 TABLET BY MOUTH EVERY DAY
tramadol 50 mg tablet
TAKE 1 TABLET BY MOUTH THREE TIMES A DAY AS NEEDED
ciprofloxacin 500 mg tablet
TAKE 1 TABLET BY MOUTH EVERY 12 HOURS FOR 3 DAYS
Flexeril 5 mg tablet
Take 1 tablet 3 times a day by oral route.
Incruse Ellipta 62.5 mcg/actuation powder for inhalation
Inhale 1 puff every day by inhalation route as directed for 90 days.
Mild chronic obstructive pulmonary disease
omeprazole 20 mg capsule,delayed release
TAKE 1 CAPSULE BY MOUTH EVERY DAY
Gastroesophageal reflux disease without esophagitis
phenazopyridine 100 mg tablet
TAKE 1 TABLET BY MOUTH THREE TIMES A DAY AFTER MEALS FOR 3 DAYS
prednisolone acetate 1 % eye drops,suspension
1 DROPS 4 TIMES A DAY IN RIGHT EYE FOR 5 DAYS
Spiriva with HandiHaler 18 mcg and inhalation capsules
Inhale 1 capsule every day by inhalation route as directed for 90 days, for Copd.

imiquimod 5 % topical cream packet

losartan 100 mg tablet

APPLY TOPICALLY TWICE DAILY TO AFFECTED AREA

APPLY TOPICALLY TWICE DAILY TO AFFECTED AREA
Take 1 tablet every day by oral route.

content.content: Patient is an 82-year-old female with past medical history significant for bilateral carpal tunnel, overweight, chronic insomnia, chronic back pain, COPD, dyslipidemia, essential hypertension, GERD, history of murmur, history of balance impairment walks with a cane assistance, history of lumbar spondylosis, osteoporosis, urinary incontinence following urology team s/p uroplasty, recent history of motor vehicle vehicle accident December 2024, hx of recent fall at home 03/2025 that presents to clinic today to follow-up on chronic medical conditions she has brought in her ambulatory blood pressure.

Since last encounter patient has seen cardiology team and is pending further workup. Reports compliance to her losartan and amlodipine. Patient denies any chest pain, shortness of breath, headache, dizziness, weakness or malaise. She has brought in her ambulatory blood pressures which are variant sometimes within goal at other times elevated. Patient is unsure what is making them go up or down.
Patient with history of tendinitis and rotator cuff injury on the left reports this has been ongoing for 3 years. She reports limitation to range of motion. Patient like to have this addressed today.
Patient has completed lab work as well like to discuss the results.

td.content: Notes:

content.content: Constitutional: General Appearance: healthy-appearing, well-developed, and overweight . Level of Distress: no acute distress. Ambulation: ambulates without assistance.
Psychiatric: Insight: good judgement. Mental Status: active and alert and normal mood. Orientation: oriented to person, place, time, time, and place.
Eyes: Lids and Conjunctivae: no discharge or pallor and non-injected. Pupils: PERRLA. EOM: EOMI. Sclerae: non-icteric.

Lungs: Respiratory effort: no dyspnea. Percussion: no dullness, flatness, or hyperresonance. Auscultation: no wheezing, rales/crackles, or rhonchi and breath sounds normal, good air movement, and CTA except as noted.

Cardiovascular: Apical Impulse: not displaced. Heart Auscultation: normal S1 and S2; no murmurs, rubs, or gallops; and RRR. Neck vessels: no carotid bruits. Pulses including femoral / pedal: normal throughout.
Musculoskeletal:: Motor Strength and Tone: normal tone and motor strength. Joints, Bones, and Muscles: normal movement of all extremities and no bony abnormalities. Extremities: no cyanosis or edema.
Neurologic: Gait and Station: normal gait and station. Coordination and Cerebellum: no tremor.
Skin: Inspection and palpation: no rash, lesions, ulcer, induration, nodules, jaundice, or abnormal nevi and good turgor. Nails: normal.

td.content: Abbreviated ROS
Constitutional:

tr.td: Constitutional: no significant weight change, good appetite, no fever, normal activity level, no fatigue

td.content: Eyes:

tr.td: Eyes: no pain, no blurry vision, no redness, no itchiness, no swelling, no discharge, normal movement

td.content: ENMT:

tr.td: ENMT: no ear pain, no ear discharge, no hearing loss, no sinus pressure, no drooling, no facial swelling, no congestion, no sore throat, no hoarseness, no mouth lesions

td.content: Cardiovascular:

tr.td: Cardiovascular: no chest pain, normal heart rate

td.content: Chest/Breasts:

tr.td: Breasts: no lumps, no tenderness, no discharge

td.content: Respiratory:

tr.td: Respiratory: no cough, no wheezing, no chest tightness, no pain with respiration, normal respiration

td.content: Gastrointestinal:

tr.td: GI: no difficulty swallowing, no abdominal pain, no nausea, no vomiting, no diarrhea, no constipation, no blood in stool, no mucous in stool

td.content: Musculoskeletal:

tr.td: Musculoskeletal: no soft tissue swelling, no joint swelling, no myalgia, moves all extremities well, no previous injuries, no trauma

td.content: Skin:

tr.td: Skin: no pain, no itchiness, no skin dryness, no flaking, no redness, no rash, no hives, no skin lesions, no skin lumps, no swelling, no bruising, no insect bites

td.content: Psychiatric:

tr.td: Psych: no depression, no anxiety, no insomnia, no stress, no loss of interest

49 mm[Hg]
66 mm[Hg]

td.content: Tobacco Smoking Status
What Is Your Level Of Alcohol Consumption?
Do you have burning pain in your legs?
Did you used to be able to walk further than you do now?
Date of last hospitalization?
Number of hospital days within current calendar year?
Chewing Tobacco:
Illicit Drug Use?
Illicit Drug Years of Use:
Do you feel safe in your home?
Cigars
Cigars?
Have you ever felt you should cut down on your drinking?
Have you ever had a drink first thing in the morning to steady your nerves or to get rid of a hangover (Eye Opener)?
Have people annoyed you by criticizing your drinking?
Have you ever felt bad or guilty about your drinking?
Does the patient have Fever and Cough or Shortness of Breath AND in the last 14 days has the patient traveled from Hubei Provience, China?
Does the patient have fever, been hospitalized in the last 14 days for lower respiratory illness and traveled from Mainland, China?
Does the patient have Fever and Cough or Shortness of Breath AND in the last 14 days has the patient traveled from Mainland China, Iran, Italy, Japan or South Korea?
Does the patient have fever and lower respiratory illness requiring hospitalization without an underlying diagnosis?
Does the patient have fever or lower respiratory symptoms such as cough or shortness of breath?
In the last 14 days, has the patient traveled to China, Iran, Italy, South Korea or Europe including the UK and Ireland?
IN the last 14 days, has the patient had close contact with a Coronavirus patient?
In the last 14 days, has the patient had close contact with a Coronavirus patient?
Does the patient have a fever?
In the last 14 days has the patient had contact with a COVID-19 positive patient or a COVID-19 suspect patient awaiting test results?
Falls?
Do you have any pets?
What type of diet are you following?
How many times per week do you exercise?
Do you have a medical power of attorney?
Has tobacco cessation counseling been provided?
What is your level of caffeine consumption?
Have there been any changes to your family or social situation?
What is the highest grade or level of school you have completed or the highest degree you have received?
What is your relationship status?
Are you sexually active?
Which of your hands is dominant?
Do you have an advance directive?
What was the date of your most recent tobacco screening?
Do you have smoke and carbon monoxide detectors in your home?
Are you passively exposed to smoke?
Do you use insect repellent routinely?
Do you use sunscreen routinely?
Are you currently in school?
Is blood transfusion acceptable in an emergency?
Birth Sex

text.paragraph: No social history survey screeners recorded

td.content: Are you able to care for yourself independently?
Are you blind or do you have difficulty seeing?
Are you deaf or do you have serious difficulty hearing?
Do you have difficulty walking or climbing stairs?
Do you have difficulty dressing, bathing, grooming, or toileting?
Do you have difficulty doing errands alone?
Do you have transportation difficulties?
What is your exercise level?
Do you or have you ever used any other forms of tobacco or nicotine?
Do you or have you ever used e-cigarettes or vape?
Do you or have you ever used smokeless tobacco?
What is your level of alcohol consumption?
Do you use any illicit or recreational drugs?
Are you currently employed?
AUDIT-C
How often did you have a drink containing alcohol in the past year?
How many drinks containing alcohol did you have on a typical day when you were drinking in the past year?
How often did you have six or more drinks on one occasion in the past year?
AUDIT-C
How often did you have a drink containing alcohol in the past year?
How many drinks containing alcohol did you have on a typical day when you were drinking in the past year?
How often did you have six or more drinks on one occasion in the past year?
STEADI Fall Risk
Have you fallen in the past year?
Do you use or have you been advised to use a cane or walker to get around safely?
Do you sometimes feel unsteady while walking?
Do you steady yourself by holding onto furniture when walking at home?
Do you worry about falling?
Do you need to push with your hands to stand up from a chair?
Do you have trouble stepping up onto a curb?
Do you often have to rush to the toilet?
Have you lost some feeling in your feet?
Do you take medicine that sometimes makes you light-headed or more tired than usual?
Do you take medicine to help you sleep or improve your mood?
Do you often feel sad or depressed?
AUDIT-C
How often did you have a drink containing alcohol in the past year?
How many drinks containing alcohol did you have on a typical day when you were drinking in the past year?
How often did you have six or more drinks on one occasion in the past year?
PEG 3-Item Pain Scale
1. What number best describes your pain on average in the past week:
2. What number best describes how, during the past week, pain has interfered with your enjoyment of life?
3. What number best describes how, during the past week, pain has interfered with your general activity?
AAFP Social Needs Tool
HOUSING
1. Are you worried or concerned that in the next two months you may not have stable housing that you own, rent, or stay in as a part of a household?
2. Think about the place you live. Do you have problems with any of the following? (select all that apply)
Bug Infestation
Mold
Lead paint or pipes
Inadequate heat
Oven or stove not working
No or not working smoke detectors
Water leaks
None of the above
FOOD
3. Within the past 12 months, you worried that your food would run out before you got money to buy more.
4. Within the past 12 months, the food you bought just didn't last and you didn't have money to get more.
TRANSPORTATION
5. Do you put off or neglect going to the doctor because of distance or transportation?
UTILITIES
6. In the past 12 months has the electric, gas, oil, or water company threatened to shut off services in your home?
CHILD CARE
7. Do problems getting child care make it difficult for you to work or study?
EMPLOYMENT
8. Do you have a job?
EDUCATION
9. Do you have a high school degree?
FINANCES
10. How often does this describe you? I don?t have enough money to pay my bills:
PERSONAL SAFETY
11. How often does anyone, including family, physically hurt you?
12. How often does anyone, including family, insult or talk down to you?
13. How often does anyone, including family, threaten you with harm?
14. How often does anyone, including family, scream or curse at you?
ASSISTANCE
15. Would you like help with any of these needs?

code.displayName: American Academy of Family Physicians Social Needs Screening Tool
1. Are you worried or concerned that in the next two months you may not have stable housing that you own, rent, or stay in as a part of a household?
2. Think about the place you live. Do you have problems with any of the following? (select all that apply)
No or not working smoke detectors
3. Within the past 12 months, you worried that your food would run out before you got money to buy more.
4. Within the past 12 months, the food you bought just didn't last and you didn't have money to get more.

6. In the past 12 months has the electric, gas, oil, or water company threatened to shut off services in your home?
7. Do problems getting child care make it difficult for you to work or study?
9. Do you have a high school degree?

12. How often does anyone, including family, insult or talk down to you?
13. How often does anyone, including family, threaten you with harm?
14. How often does anyone, including family, scream or curse at you?
15. Would you like help with any of these needs?

td.content: Do you have difficulty concentrating, remembering or making decisions?
Do you feel stressed (tense, restless, nervous, or anxious, or unable to sleep at night)?
PHQ-2/PHQ-9
Little interest or pleasure in doing things
Feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
Feeling tired or having little energy
Poor appetite or overeating
Feeling bad about yourself - or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down
Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television
Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed? Or the opposite - being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around a lot more than usual
Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself in some way

Feeling nervous, anxious or on edge
Not being able to stop or control worrying
Worrying too much about different things
Trouble relaxing
Being so restless that it is hard to sit still
Becoming easily annoyed or irritable
Feeling afraid as if something awful might happen

Geriatric Depression Scale
Are you basically satisfied with your life?
Have you dropped many of your activities and interests?
Do you feel that your life is empty?
Do you often get bored?
Are you in good spirits most of the time?
Are you afraid that something bad is going to happen to you?
Do you feel happy most of the time?
Do you often feel helpless?
Do you prefer to stay at home, rather than going out and doing new things?
Do you feel you have more problems with memory than most?
Do you think it is wonderful to be alive now?
Do you feel pretty worthless the way you are now?
Do you feel full of energy?
Do you feel that your situation is hopeless?
Do you think that most people are better off than you are?

Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed? Or the opposite - being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around a lot more than usual
Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself in some way
If you checked off any problems, how difficult have these problems made it for you to do your work, take care of things at home, or get along with other people?
PHQ-2/PHQ-9
Little interest or pleasure in doing things
Feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
Feeling tired or having little energy
Poor appetite or overeating
Feeling bad about yourself - or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down
Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television
Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed? Or the opposite - being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around a lot more than usual
Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself in some way
If you checked off any problems, how difficult have these problems made it for you to do your work, take care of things at home, or get along with other people?
PHQ-2/PHQ-9
Little interest or pleasure in doing things
Feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
Feeling tired or having little energy
Poor appetite or overeating
Feeling bad about yourself - or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down
Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television
Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed? Or the opposite - being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around a lot more than usual
Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself in some way
If you checked off any problems, how difficult have these problems made it for you to do your work, take care of things at home, or get along with other people?
GAD-7
Over the last two weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problems?
Feeling nervous, anxious or on edge
Not being able to stop or control worrying
Worrying too much about different things
Trouble relaxing
Being so restless that it is hard to sit still
Becoming easily annoyed or irritable
Feeling afraid as if something awful might happen

code.displayName: COVID-19, mRNA, LNP-S, PF, 100 mcg/0.5 mL dose (Moderna)

tr.td:
VALLEY HEALTH MESA CLINIC
4500 E Main St.,#225 MESA, CA 85201

td.content: Discussed with patient that blood pressure is not within goal.
Patient brought ambulatory BP, blood pressure varies from 120s-160 systolic and diastolic 60-80s.

Patient has a couple of blood pressures in the 150s as well noted on her ambulatory blood pressure in 140s. Patient was referred to cardiology due to the variance in her blood pressures I did review the notes from Dr. Bhakta reports that he will do echocardiogram and further workup. Patient today reports that she did complete the echocardiogram and was told that she had possible LVH but no further workup was needed but follow-up in a year with cardiology. Recommendations to continue with current management of losartan and amlodipine. Continued monitoring blood pressures at home. Will determine if we need to increase the amlodipine at next encounter. Patient has gained some weight since last encounter and she will work on dietary changes to see if this also makes any effect on her blood pressure. Will follow-up with patient in the next 5 months.
Patient is to bring ambulatory blood pressures at next encounter.
Emphasized etiology and management of HTN, compliance emphasized.
Also discussed associated cardiac and endocrine health risks.
Recommended that patient continue on Losartan 100 mg and amlodipine 5 mg.
Patient denies any side effects secondary to the medication, refills given.

Stressed to patient of importance of exercise that includes minimum 45 minutes 3-4 times a week, in addition to diet. Diet should include healthy choices such as low in fat, decrease carbohydrates, and increase vegetables, protein, and fruits.
Also reminded patient of importance of avoiding medications that increase blood pressure like NSAIDs/antihistamines/decongestants.
Stressed the importance of follow up for hypertension to help maintain control.
Will follow up with patient at next encounter.
Handouts given at check out.
Patient noted to have hemoglobin A1c of 5.9% , which indicated pre-diabetic state.
Discussed with patient etiology and management of prediabetes.
Also discussed with patient health risks associated with prediabetes.
Lifestyle and dietary modifications discussed.
We'll follow-up with patient in subsequent encounters.
Negative screening.
Patient with BMI of 25 indicating overweight.
Patient aware of health risks associated with overweight.
Diet, exercise, and lifestyle changes advised.
Will FU next encounter.
Handouts given at check out.

content.content: Patient reporting 3 years of chronic left shoulder tendinitis due to rotator cuff injury that occurred 3 years ago. She reports aggravation with the motor vehicle accident that she had previously. Patient reports ongoing limitations range of motion on exam. Referral to PT was placed we will try to see if we can improve conservatively if no improvement or worsening, we will be placing a referral to the orthopedic with MRI.

td.content: Patient is agreeable to the above plan.

tr.td: Screening for malignant neoplasm of colon

td.content: Patient reports he is due for colonoscopy screening she like to discuss with GI whether she like to continue with ongoing colonoscopy versus other surveillance. Referral was placed today.

playingEntity.name: VALLEY HEALTH PARTNERS LLC - VALLEY HEALTH MESA CLINIC

value.displayName: Screening for malignant neoplasm of colon

translation.displayName: Encounter for screening for malignant neoplasm of colon

td.content: Patient is an 82-year-old female with past medical history significant for bilateral carpal tunnel, overweight, chronic insomnia, chronic back pain, COPD, dyslipidemia, essential hypertension, GERD, history of murmur, history of balance impairment walks with a cane assistance, history of lumbar spondylosis, osteoporosis, urinary incontinence following urology team s/p uroplasty, recent history of motor vehicle vehicle accident December 2024, hx of recent fall at home 03/2025 that presents to clinic today to follow-up on chronic medical conditions she has brought in her ambulatory blood pressure.

Since last encounter patient has seen cardiology team and is pending further workup. Reports compliance to her losartan and amlodipine. Patient denies any chest pain, shortness of breath, headache, dizziness, weakness or malaise. She has brought in her ambulatory blood pressures which are variant sometimes within goal at other times elevated. Patient is unsure what is making them go up or down.
Patient with history of tendinitis and rotator cuff injury on the left reports this has been ongoing for 3 years. She reports limitation to range of motion. Patient like to have this addressed today.
Patient has completed lab work as well like to discuss the results.

tr.td:
Maria Santos, MD
789 Medical Plaza,STE 225, Springfield, CA, 62703, US
100 Oak Ave, Ste A, Lakewood, WA
